name: integrations
description: Two-lane integration substrate per ADR 0025. Lane 1 (SHIPS NOW) — allowlisted native provider integrations via `IntegrationProvider` (allowlist catalog) + `TenantIntegration` (per-tenant configured instance). Providers auto-discovered via `#[AsIntegrationProvider(kind, slug)]` — Stripe, Paddle, Pipedrive, HubSpot, Twilio, Zoom, Google Calendar, Apple Wallet, Google Wallet, Mailchimp, Xero, and every other native integration. Lane 2 (SCHEMA ONLY, MARKETPLACE DEFERRED) — `App` + `AppInstallation` + `AppWebhookSubscription` model the future third-party marketplace. Data model lands day-one to avoid retrofit; no third-party accepted until compliance + review process ships. First-party partner apps may be seeded with `is_first_party: true`. Enhanced consent flow required for any app requesting child-data scopes (athletes / medical / attendance / safeguarding).

attribute_driven.description: Two class-level attributes drive discovery. IntegrationProviderRegistry hydrated at boot via Foundation's DiscoversAttributes seam. Adding a Lane 1 provider = new class with #[AsIntegrationProvider] + a code deploy — no schema change.

attributes.AsIntegrationProvider: Marks a class as a Lane 1 integration provider. `#[AsIntegrationProvider(kind: 'crm', slug: 'pipedrive')]`. Class implements `IntegrationProviderInterface` — configureCredentials / verifyConnection / sync / verifyWebhookSignature / handleWebhook / rotateCredentials. Discovered at boot; IntegrationProviderSeeder upserts the matching `integration_providers` row.
attributes.AsAppExtensionPoint: Declares an extension point that Lane 2 apps may subscribe to. `#[AsAppExtensionPoint(event: 'registrations.enrolled', requiredScopes: ['athletes:read'])]`. Documentation-only until marketplace launches; when apps are accepted, this attribute drives the scope-check + webhook delivery path.

lanes.description: See ADR 0025 for the full two-lane rationale.

lane_1_providers.extension_mechanism: #[AsIntegrationProvider(kind, slug)] on a PHP class implementing IntegrationProviderInterface. Class ships in a package deployed by Stackra or a vetted partner via PR.

lane_2_marketplace.shape: App (marketplace catalog) + AppInstallation (per-tenant grant) + AppWebhookSubscription (per-install event feed)
explicitly_deferred: per-app billing + revenue share (uses finance/subscription when built)
iframe sandbox / module federation extension host
lane_2_marketplace.child_data_gate: Apps requesting scopes that read/write minor data (athletes / medical / attendance / safeguarding) require: enhanced app review (compliance sign-off), explicit per-install consent flow, COPPA/GDPR data-processing addendum, per-access audit log.

platform-admin.description: Stackra staff surface. Provider catalog CRUD (system rows; guarded), app review, cross-tenant install oversight. All secrets always redacted on read.

tenant.description: Tenant admin surface. Browse the provider catalog, configure integrations, install first-party apps (until marketplace launches), manage webhooks + revoke access.

central.description: OAuth 2.0 callback + webhook receiver surface. Per-provider callback paths + a generic webhook receiver signed by the provider's shared secret.
